title: ranunculus cymbalaria subsp. sarmentosus
intro: Ranunculus cymbalaria subsp. sarmentosus, also known as sea violet or creeping bellflower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as alkaloids and saponins. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, and it has been traditionally used to treat coughs and colds. Ranunculus cymbalaria subsp. sarmentosus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
